issues: Headline 'Richmond and Humboldt set for showdown' is not found verbatim in the OCR; the actual implied headline from the OCR content is more accurately derived from the opening question/narrative, but the draft headline is likely AI-fabricated — no clear bold headline is present in the OCR text for the Sockeyes article.
Score in the 'At a glance' box reads 'Richmond 4 Pembroke 3', not 4-1 as stated in both the short and long descriptions. The draft incorrectly states the score as 4-1.

The long description attributes a quote about killing penalties to Kurtenbach but does not mention Steve Jaques, who is the primary named quote source in the OCR and whose quote is the article's lead.

The long description omits the key narrative framing: the article is primarily about the upcoming Richmond vs. Humboldt showdown, built around their prior seven-game series rivalry, not just a recap of the Pembroke game.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Richmond Sockeyes beat Pembroke 4-3 to go 2-0 in Centennial Cup round robin, setting up a winner-take-all showdown with Humboldt Broncos on Wednesday.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: The Richmond Sockeyes defeated the Pembroke Lumber Kings 4-3 on Monday to move to 2-0 in the Centennial Cup round robin at the Humboldt Uniplex, tying Humboldt Broncos atop the standings. Dave Tomlinson scored two third-period goals to seal the win, while coach Orland Kurtenbach credited the team for killing numerous penalties. The win sets up a decisive Wednesday clash between Richmond and Humboldt — teams with a heated prior seven-game series rivalry — with the winner earning an automatic berth in Saturday's championship final. Defenceman Steve Jaques summed up the mood: "We know who we have to beat to win the Centennial Cup."
review.notes: No clear standalone headline is visible in the OCR for the Sockeyes article — the draft headline appears AI-fabricated. The scoreline in the 'At a glance' box clearly reads 4-3, not 4-1 as the draft states. Editor should confirm the article's actual headline from the original image.
